newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Fresh items are commentary only: a Seeking Alpha piece arguing the data-center premium (~26x forward EPS) is not supported by fundamentals, alongside earlier analyst PT moves (Stifel to $379 Buy, Barclays to $323 Equal Weight). The $0.63 quarterly dividend (payable June 15) was declared in May; Q1 record results (+21% revenue, EPS +34%, data-center-driven) and the Haws/Superior Boiler/Saudi Cast acquisitions predate the window. Day move +0.23% is drift.

sources.label: Seeking Alpha - Data Center Angle Isn't Enough To Support This Valuation
StockTitan - Watts Water Q1 EPS Rises 34%, Keeps 2026 Outlook
Business Wire - Watts Water Declares Quarterly Dividend
Yahoo Finance - Bull Case For WTS Could Change Following AI Data Center Cooling Expansion

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already repriced the adjusted-score gap; with no new evidence since June 6 and a +0.23% drift day, another move is unjustified. Small -1 for crowding drift: the data-center water/cooling angle is now widely covered by sell-side and retail media, eroding the 'quiet compounder' element of the latent-node thesis.
verdicts.bull: Data-center demand is showing up in real numbers (Q1 +21% revenue, EPS +34%) and water stewardship for high-density AI campuses remains an under-modeled, structural bottleneck Watts directly serves.
verdicts.neutral: At ~26x forward and consensus Hold with PT ~$333 vs $316, the easy rerating is largely done; the name compounds but residual alpha now depends on data-center revenue mix growing faster than the multiple implies.
verdicts.bear: The data-center narrative is becoming consensus on a stock that is mostly plumbing/commercial repair-and-replace; if AI capex cools or the data-center mix disappoints, the premium multiple compresses against conservative full-year guidance.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings showing data-center/cooling revenue acceleration or a guidance raise off the conservative 8-12% sales outlook.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression as the now-popular data-center angle fails to move the revenue needle on a $9.9B diversified flow-control business.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict-screen pathway (valves, backflow prevention, drainage, water quality adapted to high-density facility water management) is unchanged; yesterday was a hold and nothing since June 6 alters the adapted-use evidence or AI-chain risk profile.
verdicts.bull: Q1 confirmed data-center growth is already flowing through organic sales, validating the adaptation pathway from generic flow/water-safety hardware to AI facility water management.
verdicts.neutral: Rank 44 on the strict list already reflects medium AI-chain attribution; no contracts or design wins specific to AI facilities were announced this window, so the evidence base is static.
verdicts.bear: Watts' AI exposure remains a thin slice of a broad plumbing/HVAC portfolio, and on a strict attribution lens the name could slide if purer facility-water plays surface with documented AI campus wins.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosed AI/data-center facility contract wins or segment-level disclosure quantifying data-center water revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Attribution risk: data-center demand proves too small a mix to distinguish WTS from generic construction-cycle exposure.
